summary: Full E2E testing of Energeide blog/admin feature + regression. Backend: 11/11 pytest pass (admin login, posts CRUD with X-Admin-Token, projects regression). Frontend: home logo contained in 88px header (bottom 72 vs header 88), no Facebook section/embed on Home or News, all historical sections present (Hero, Stats, Pricing, Suggestion 9 kW, Why Us, Testimonials, CTA), /news shows blog-posts-grid, /admin login/create/list/delete/logout flow all working, address 'Corso Vittorio' / 'Quadrelle' completely removed (Home, News, Footer, Contatti), /contatti still shows +39 350 931 2938 and info@energeide.it.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: single file ~330 lines, still maintainable but if more endpoints are added consider splitting routers (projects/, blog/, admin/) into separate modules.
Admin auth uses a single shared static token in env. Acceptable for a 1-admin showcase site, but if admin team grows, switch to per-user JWT login.
Frontend Admin.jsx stores token in localStorage; this is XSS-exposed. Fine for low-risk MVP, but consider httpOnly cookie or shorter session for production.
News.jsx fetches posts every navigation without revalidation indicator beyond skeleton; minor UX polish only.
seed_data_creation: Test post created and then deleted during the E2E run; DB left clean.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_blog_admin.py covers blog CRUD + admin login + projects regression. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_blog_admin.py -v. Admin token lives in backend/.env (ADMIN_TOKEN) and must match. Frontend pages: /admin form posts to /api/admin/login then stores token in localStorage 'energeide_admin_token'; subsequent POST /api/posts and DELETE /api/posts/{id} use X-Admin-Token header. /news fetches /api/posts (public).
rca of the issue: Not applicable - no failures observed. All requested behaviors verified: (1) logo height (h-10 sm:h-12 md:h-14 ≈ 40-56px) fits inside h-[88px] header; (2) FacebookFeed component fully removed from Home and News; (3) address strings absent everywhere; (4) blog admin auth via X-Admin-Token correctly returns 401 on missing/wrong token and 200 on correct token; (5) public GET /api/posts returns the published list; (6) projects endpoint still 200.
